title: D4: the two bee-hive reference files stop teaching the retired reading order
action: `skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md:20` explicitly sends the agent to these two files for "the full routing table" and "the full pipeline". Measured today: `references/routing-and-contracts.md` contains `docs/knowledge` 0 times and `docs/specs` 4 times; `references/go-mode.md` 0 and 1. An agent that follows the FULL instructions is taught the model G4 replaced, while the SKILL two files away teaches the opposite. Bring both to parity with `bee-hive/SKILL.md`'s own bundleMode branch (read it first, at SKILL.md:69-76, and MIRROR its two-branch shape and its wording — do not invent a third phrasing of the same law). Named passages, each of which must gain the both-branches treatment: (routing-and-contracts.md) the State Bootstrap line telling the agent to read critical-patterns.md; the Scout Contract table row naming `docs/specs/<area>.md` as the area surface; the per-area reading-order line that reads `spec -> decision index -> history`; the Chaining Contract table's scribing row naming `docs/specs/<area>.md` as its output; and the File Quick Reference, which lists NO bundle path at all. (go-mode.md) the `[BOOTSTRAP]` step's critical-patterns mention, and `[STEP 6]`'s `bee-scribing -> docs/specs/<area>.md BA-grade sync`, which directly contradicts AGENTS.md:33. In EVERY case the no-bundle branch must state today's guidance unchanged and unqualified — a host repo that never migrated must not be able to tell this release happened (G1). Also state, once, where each tree's truth lives now: the bundle is current truth; `docs/specs/` is the READ-ONLY compatibility surface whose stubs resolve legacy `#R7`-style anchor citations. Do NOT rewrite either file wholesale and do NOT restate the bundle predicate in new prose of your own — cite `bundleMode` the way the SKILL does.

truths: grep -c 'docs/knowledge' on both files returns > 0, and every added passage carries BOTH branches (bundle / no bundle)
the no-bundle branch of every touched passage still states today's guidance verbatim-equivalent, so a never-migrated host reads unchanged instructions
no passage in either file still tells the agent that docs/specs/<area>.md is where current area truth lives when a bundle exists
go-mode.md STEP 6 no longer names docs/specs/<area>.md as scribing's unconditional output
prohibitions: no wholesale rewrite of either file — touch the named passages and what they contradict
never restate the bundle predicate as new prose; cite bundleMode as bee-hive/SKILL.md does
no new content written under docs/specs/ (D8)
do NOT regenerate the plugin trees, the release manifest, the .bee/bin mirror or the onboarding ledger — f4-3 owns all four for the whole feature (D9)

trace.outcome: D4 closed: the two reference files bee-hive/SKILL.md defers to for the full routing table and the full pipeline no longer teach the retired reading order. Measured 0 -> 5 and 0 -> 2 occurrences of docs/knowledge. Every named contradicting passage now carries both branches in the SKILL's own shape and wording — State Bootstrap's critical-patterns read, the Scout Contract's per-lane area row, the per-area reading order, the Chaining Contract's scribing output, the File Quick Reference (which listed no bundle path at all), go-mode's BOOTSTRAP step and its STEP 6. Every no-bundle branch states today's guidance unchanged, so a never-migrated host cannot tell this release happened (G1). Orchestrator corrected one inaccuracy before accept: the quick reference globbed concepts as concept-*.md, which is not how they are named.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "red_failure_evidence": "BEFORE (measured on HEAD, git show HEAD:<file> | grep -c 'docs/knowledge'): routing-and-contracts.md = 0, go-mode.md = 0 — while the same files carried 'docs/specs' 4 and 1 times respectively. These are the two files bee-hive/SKILL.md:20 explicitly defers to for 'the full routing table' and 'the full pipeline', so an agent following the FULL instructions was taught: State Bootstrap 'Read docs/history/learnings/critical-patterns.md' (a retired pointer stub) with no bundle branch; a per-area reading order of 'spec -> decision index -> history', flatly contradicting bee-hive/SKILL.md's own bundleMode branch two files away; a Chaining Contract whose scribing row output was 'docs/specs/<area>.md' only; a File Quick Reference listing NO bundle path at all; and go-mode STEP 6 'bee-scribing -> docs/specs/<area>.md BA-grade sync', contradicting AGENTS.md:33. No suite was red for any of this — that is the silent-rot failure class, and it is why the before-state is a count, not a stack trace.",
  "after": "AFTER: routing-and-contracts.md = 5 occurrences of docs/knowledge, go-mode.md = 2. Every named passage now carries BOTH branches in the same shape and wording bee-hive/SKILL.md already uses, and every no-bundle branch states today's guidance unchanged so a never-migrated host cannot tell this release happened (G1).",
  "orchestrator_review": "Diff read in full by the orchestrator, not accepted on the worker's word. One inaccuracy found and corrected by the orchestrator before accept: the new File Quick Reference entry globbed concepts as 'concept-*.md', which is not how concepts are named — corrected to '<subject-slug>.md' and extended to name patterns/ and work/<id>/ so the quick reference matches the real bundle shape.",
  "chain": "node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> PASS 79 suite(s), exit 0, run SERIALLY by the orchestrator after both sibling workers were off the tree and after the plugin-tree render + release-manifest write.",
  "transient_red_resolved": "Both workers reported test_guards.mjs and/or test_hook_contracts.mjs red during the swarm. Confirmed transient: w-refs re-ran them standalone and on a full re-run and they passed, and the orchestrator's own serial full-chain run after the swarm is green on all 79. This is the known 'a shared suite red is not yours while another agent is mid-write' pattern — two workers were running the full chain concurrently."
}
